text: A yield sign means:
options: Slow down and let traffic with the right-of-way go first

text: A steady yellow traffic light means:
options: The light is about to turn red; stop if you can do so safely
Speed up to beat the light

text: A flashing red traffic light means:
options: Treat it like a stop sign — stop, then go when clear
The light is out of order; ignore it

text: A flashing yellow traffic light means:
options: Slow down and proceed with caution
The same as a red light

text: A green arrow at an intersection means:
options: You may turn in the arrow's direction; oncoming traffic is stopped
Yield to all oncoming traffic before turning

options: Allowed after a complete stop, unless a sign prohibits it

text: A red arrow means:
options: Stop; do not turn in that direction until it changes

text: A steady green light means:
options: Go through the intersection if it is clear

text: When turning left at a steady green light you must:
options: Yield to oncoming traffic and pedestrians
Wait for a green arrow every time

options: Be ready to stop in case it changes

text: A solid yellow line on your side of the centerline means:

text: Two solid yellow lines between lanes mean:
options: Neither direction may pass (cross only to turn)

text: A dashed white line between lanes means:
options: You may change lanes when safe

text: A solid white line between lanes means:
options: Stay in your lane unless a special situation requires a change

text: White lane markings separate:
options: Lanes of traffic moving in the same direction

text: In a reversible lane, a red 'X' over the lane means:
options: The lane is not available to you

text: A white diamond painted in a lane usually marks:
options: A reserved lane (HOV, bus, or bike)

The car on the left goes first

options: The driver who reaches the intersection first

options: Have the right-of-way; pull right and stop

A dashed yellow line on your side

options: Keep right and maintain a steady speed

options: Never allowed (except when directed by authorities)

options: Any amount of alcohol is illegal

options: Find a safe place to take a 15 to 20 minute nap
Open the window and speed up

options: Get out of their way and avoid eye contact
Challenge them to hold your position

Wear belts only on long trips

options: Always, even in vehicles with air bags
Only if there are no air bags

In the middle of the back seat

options: Have the same rights and responsibilities as drivers

options: Keep as far to the right as possible
Stay in the center of your lane

options: Whenever your windshield wipers are being used

text: At night you should dim your high beams when an oncoming vehicle is within about:

options: About twice as far as low beams

options: At least two blocks or two traffic signals

options: A pedestrian or bicyclist may be moving into your path
Telling a driver they made an error

text: You should NOT use your horn:
options: When a crash is about to happen
When someone is drifting into your lane

options: Get off the road, turn on hazard flashers, and warn others
Stay in the travel lane with the engine running

options: Make sure your turn signal has canceled

options: Make sure the parking brake is set
Place your foot on the accelerator

options: Place your foot on the accelerator

options: One hand at the bottom of the wheel

options: Turn and look over your shoulder through the rear window

options: Contact the back of your head, not below your ears
Touch the top of your head

options: Brake and steer at the same time without skidding

options: Press the brake firmly and keep holding it

text: When adjusting your seat, the top of the steering wheel should be:

options: It first starts to rain, snow, or sleet
It has been raining for a while

options: Reduce your speed and increase your following distance

options: Turn around and find another route

Run in the same direction the train is traveling

text: When approaching an animal standing near the roadway, you should:
options: Speed up to scare it away

text: If a tire suddenly blows out, you should:
options: Grip the wheel, ease off the gas, and not brake hard

text: If your vehicle begins to lose traction (skid), you should:
options: Ease off the pedals and steer where you want to go
Brake hard to stop the skid

text: If your brakes stop working you should first:
options: Pump the brake pedal rapidly to build pressure

options: Freeze before other parts of the road

options: Leave the vehicle for sturdy shelter

Speed up to push through the water

options: When it is dark, in poor visibility, and whenever your wipers are on

options: Extend your arm straight out to the side, horizontally
Bend your arm upward at the elbow
Bend your arm downward at the elbow

options: Extend your arm out and bend it upward at the elbow (hand pointing up)
Extend your arm straight out horizontally

options: Extend your arm out and bend it downward at the elbow (hand pointing down)
Extend your arm straight out horizontally
Bend your arm upward at the elbow
